title: Why did Instrumentality need to be proposed by Gendo
display_title: Why did Instrumentality need to be proposed by Gendo
wikitext:
[]: Why did Instrumentality need to be proposed by Gendo?             Quote Post by Reichu » Thu Dec 13, 2007 2:52 pm Seele is an organization of religious zealots obsessed with mysticism and alien technology / scripture. The CI seems to explicitly indicate that achieving immortality, apparently by returning to God and a primordial state of being ("Adam Kadmon"), has been a long-standing dream of the organization (albeit one that was forgotten for a while, up till the discovery of the SDSS) . The White Moon was uncovered and Second Impact (well... at least some aspects of it) engineered, we can only assume, as part of an ongoing plan. So why on Earth does Gendo need to propose the Human Instrumentality Project (= The Path to God) to Kiel and the others in 2005? Isn't that the sort of thing Seele has been working towards all along? This has been come up before, but I can't recall any explanation that satisfied me.

[]:             Quote Post by BobBQ » Thu Dec 13, 2007 2:53 pm He may have proposed a specific solution to a general question, or something of that sort.

[]:             Quote Post by THE Hal E. Burton 9000 » Thu Dec 13, 2007 2:53 pm PLOT HOLE, GENIUS :P

[]:             Quote Post by BobBQ » Thu Dec 13, 2007 2:57 pm THE Hal E. Burton 9000 wrote: PLOT HOLE, GENIUS :P

[]:             Quote Post by jenova7 » Thu Dec 13, 2007 2:57 pm SEELE was searching to become one with god, but didn't have the way to manifest that goal until gendo thought up instrumentality

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Thu Dec 13, 2007 3:04 pm jenova7 wrote: SEELE was searching to become one with god, but didn't have the way to manifest that goal until gendo thought up instrumentality

[]:             Quote Post by fadingreminder » Thu Dec 13, 2007 3:30 pm Dammit all... Now I've got a headache... Very good question, now I'm curious too.

[]:             Quote Post by Defectron » Thu Dec 13, 2007 4:00 pm Why were they creating Evas, whose intrinsic purpose is to "open the Path to God"? (The Angel-killing thing is explicitly secondary.) Why would they even bother with the White Moon and Adam if they didn't have a plan?

[]:             Quote Post by Ironfoot » Thu Dec 13, 2007 4:11 pm Gendo wanted to get into his wife's organization from the onset, and the proposal of such a project would solidify his position in SEELE. Therefore, it is possible that SEELE could not have known how to achieve immortality using the Evangelion at that point, considering how little was known about it at the time. He offered the plan to SEELE, yet kept his real plans close to his chest until the time came right. SEELE diverted from the original plan (Gendo's) during the series, so Gendo decided to use the Lance, etc.

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Thu Dec 13, 2007 5:10 pm Just to make sure I understand the question, are you asking just WHY Gendou had any part of it when Seele could have set Instrumentality in motion without his "help" (*cough* Backstabber! *cough*)?? My guess is it probably had to do with Yui being inside of Shogoki, but other than that "I dunno, lol."

[]:             Quote Post by Themaninblack » Thu Dec 13, 2007 5:18 pm The bigger question is....why betray them if they where following his plans in the first place

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Thu Dec 13, 2007 5:35 pm Seele's and Gendou's versions were different, though. eg, Seele in Episode 25': "All living things will be united in death." (Implying that's the type of Instrumentality they wanted.) Gendou's reply: "Death gives life to nothing." (Implying Gendou's all like, "lol, no" about their version.) ....I know enough about this show as is. I'm gonna be an EXTREME Eva-nerd by the time this site's finished, aren't I?

[]:             Quote Post by BrikHaus » Thu Dec 13, 2007 6:22 pm Sailor Star Dust wrote: I'm gonna be an EXTREME Eva-nerd by the time this site's finished, aren't I? [/size]

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Thu Dec 13, 2007 6:39 pm * Hugs Brik-kun * You're OUR forum asshole, so there!

[]:             Quote Post by THE Hal E. Burton 9000 » Thu Dec 13, 2007 6:44 pm hey, this isn't the first plot hole in Eva

[]:             Quote Post by BrikHaus » Thu Dec 13, 2007 6:48 pm Sailor Star Dust wrote: * Hugs Brik-kun * You're OUR forum asshole, so there!

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Thu Dec 13, 2007 7:17 pm Themaninblack wrote: The bigger question is....why betray them if they where following his plans in the first place

[]:             Quote Post by UrsusArctos » Thu Dec 13, 2007 7:32 pm Well, Yui and the Akagis are a little too brilliant in the show's context, methinks. What about the so many associate or assistant researchers who would be helping in a project of this scale? There are people of the Akagis' caliber if not higher, as the MP Evas prove. As for Gendo proposing the HIP, Gendo would have known a lot from Yui and Naoko even if he were not a scientist. If he understood what an Eva or SoL was capable of, he could report to Seele and propose instrumentality. Seele's path to God was inherently flawed because it relied on what Gendo told them-that evolving mankind to God was possible only through 3I/Instrumentality. Gendo himself rejects the idea, saying that "Death brings about nothing", while Seele is all for it. Gendo wants to reunite with Yui, no matter what. And, in the end, Gendo is reunited, but not without a little head-biting. All of HIP was Gendo's setup, to get back to Yui. It was all his. As for the destruction of the Angels being purely secondary, I'm sure it was because Seele wanted to get as much as they wanted out of them first.

[]:             Quote Post by Sachi » Thu Dec 13, 2007 8:05 pm maybe Gendo totally lied to SEELE after finding out about instrumentality, and to get SEELE to attempt to start intrumentality, but since Gendo gave them totaly bullshit from the total bullshit that (perhaps) Yui told/ left behind for him and everything came to Yui's orignal idea of instrumentality

[]:             Quote Post by TemporalKnight » Thu Dec 13, 2007 9:17 pm Perhaps SEELE planned on initiating 3I and having everybody go back to Lilith thereby becoming a "God" but this originally didn't include the "giant-mind-meld-in-the-sky." That part possibly would only come about by the inclusion of certain aspects (got me on what fanwank some stuff...maybe like the Adam or some such). And then Gendo figured out, with the assitance of Yui, what that stuff would be and figured SEELE would be all for it. At first then SEELE thought it was a good idea but then Gendo started messing up little aspects and they decided to go with their original plan prompting Gendo to say that stuff about "Death brings to life nothing" since that version is the end of all consciousness not just individual consciousness.

So why on Earth does Gendo need to propose the Human Instrumentality Project (= The Path to God) to Kiel and the others in 2005? Isn't that the sort of thing Seele has been working towards all along? This has been come up before, but I can't recall any explanation that satisfied me.
